title: Writing a story with linkers
explanation:
Writing a story with linkers

At B1 level, stories are easier to follow when you use time linkers and sequence words such as suddenly, while, afterwards, eventually, in the end. Use past simple for main events and past continuous for background action.

questions.text: [*While I was waiting for the bus, I noticed a wallet on the ground.|While waiting bus I noticed wallet.|I noticed wallet while wait bus.]
[*Suddenly, a man ran towards me and asked if I had seen his wallet.|Suddenly a man run to me ask wallet.|A man suddenly wallet ask.]
[*In the end, I returned it and he thanked me warmly.|In the end I return it he thank me.|End I returned and thanked.]
[*Afterwards, I felt proud that I had done the right thing.|Afterwards I proud I right thing done.|Proud afterwards right thing.]

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Use the past ____ for the main actions in a story. | *simple | continuous only | perfect always
questions.feedback: Main events usually use past simple.

questions.text: Words like suddenly and eventually help show the ____.  | *sequence | forecast | receipt
A story often becomes more interesting when something ____.  | *unexpected happens | formal appears | passive is used
The final sentence can express how the writer ____.  | *felt | resigned | broadcast
questions.feedback: A story can end with a feeling.

questions.text: [*At first, I thought someone had dropped it by mistake.|At first I think someone drop it.|First I thought someone mistake.]
[*A few minutes later, the owner arrived looking worried.|Few minutes later owner arrived worry.|Owner arrived worry few minutes.]
[*Because I had kept the wallet safe, nothing was missing.|Because I kept wallet safe nothing missing was.|Wallet safe because nothing.]
[*It was an ordinary day that turned into a memorable experience.|It was ordinary day into memorable.|Ordinary day memorable experience turned.]
